title: Central European Time

infobox.value: Legal time vs local mean time

(**CET**) is a [standard time](/wiki/Standard_time "Standard time") observed in [Central](/wiki/Central_Europe "Central Europe") as well as parts of [Western](/wiki/Western_Europe "Western Europe") and [Southeast Europe](/wiki/Southeast_Europe "Southeast Europe"), which is one hour ahead of [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time") (UTC). The [time offset from UTC](/wiki/UTC_offset "UTC offset") can be written as [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00"). It covers most of continental [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe") and it has been adopted by several [African](/wiki/Africa "Africa") countries where it is known under various other names.

(MET, German: [MEZ](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mitteleurop%C3%A4ische_Zeit "de:Mitteleuropäische Zeit")) and by colloquial names that reference major European cities such as **Amsterdam Time**, **Berlin Time**, **Brussels Time**, **Budapest Time**, **Madrid Time**, **Paris Time**, **Stockholm Time**, **Rome Time**, **Prague time**, **Warsaw Time** or **Romance Standard Time** (RST).

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Standard time (UTC+01:00)

| Central European Time | |
| --- | --- |

| UTC offset | |
| CET | [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") |
| CEST | [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") |
| Current time | |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/78/UTC%2B1_time_zones_in_africa_plus_longitude_15%C2%B0_east_from_greenwich.png/250px-UTC%2B1_time_zones_in_africa_plus_longitude_15%C2%B0_east_from_greenwich.png)](/wiki/File:UTC%2B1_time_zones_in_africa_plus_longitude_15%C2%B0_east_from_greenwich.png)

On the map; countries marked red in Africa use the West African Time zone (WAT) that is identical to the CET, because it is also based on the longitude 15° E

**Central European Time** (**CET**) is a [standard time](/wiki/Standard_time "Standard time") observed in [Central](/wiki/Central_Europe "Central Europe") as well as parts of [Western](/wiki/Western_Europe "Western Europe") and [Southeast Europe](/wiki/Southeast_Europe "Southeast Europe"), which is one hour ahead of [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time") (UTC). The [time offset from UTC](/wiki/UTC_offset "UTC offset") can be written as [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00"). It covers most of continental [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe") and it has been adopted by several [African](/wiki/Africa "Africa") countries where it is known under various other names.

CET is also known as **Middle European Time** (MET, German: [MEZ](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mitteleurop%C3%A4ische_Zeit "de:Mitteleuropäische Zeit")) and by colloquial names that reference major European cities such as **Amsterdam Time**, **Berlin Time**, **Brussels Time**, **Budapest Time**, **Madrid Time**, **Paris Time**, **Stockholm Time**, **Rome Time**, **Prague time**, **Warsaw Time** or **Romance Standard Time** (RST).

The [15th meridian east](/wiki/15th_meridian_east "15th meridian east") is the central axis per [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") in the world system of [time zones](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone").

Currently, all [member countries](/wiki/Member_state_of_the_European_Union "Member state of the European Union") of the [European Union](/wiki/European_Union "European Union") observe summer time ([daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time")), from the last Sunday in March to the last Sunday in October.
Countries within the CET area switch to [Central European Summer Time](/wiki/Central_European_Summer_Time "Central European Summer Time") (CEST, [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")) for the summer.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
The next change to CET will occur on 25 October 2026 (switching from 03:00 CEST to 02:00 CET).

In Africa, [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") is called [West Africa Time](/wiki/West_Africa_Time "West Africa Time") (WAT), where it is used by several countries, year round.[[2]](#cite_note-2)
[Algeria](/wiki/Algeria "Algeria"), [Morocco](/wiki/Morocco "Morocco"), and [Tunisia](/wiki/Tunisia "Tunisia") also refer to it as *Central European Time*.[[3]](#cite_note-cetusage-3)

## Usage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Time&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Usage")]

### Usage in Europe

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Time&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Usage in Europe")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/Stargard_15_Po%C5%82udnik.JPG/250px-Stargard_15_Po%C5%82udnik.JPG)](/wiki/File:Stargard_15_Po%C5%82udnik.JPG)

The '15th Meridian' monument in [Stargard](/wiki/Stargard "Stargard"), Poland

#### Current usage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Time&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Current usage")]

As of 2017,[[4]](#cite_note-4) Central European Time is currently used in [Albania](/wiki/Albania "Albania"), [Andorra](/wiki/Andorra "Andorra"), [Austria](/wiki/Austria "Austria"), [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium"), [Bosnia and Herzegovina](/wiki/Time_in_Bosnia_and_Herzegovina "Time in Bosnia and Herzegovina"), [Croatia](/wiki/Croatia "Croatia"), [Czechia](/wiki/Time_in_the_Czech_Republic "Time in the Czech Republic"), [Denmark](/wiki/Time_in_Denmark "Time in Denmark"), [France](/wiki/Time_in_France "Time in France"), [Germany](/wiki/Time_in_Germany "Time in Germany"), [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar") (British territory), [Hungary](/wiki/Time_in_Hungary "Time in Hungary"), [Italy](/wiki/Time_in_Italy "Time in Italy"), [Kosovo](/wiki/Kosovo "Kosovo") (*[partially recognised](/wiki/International_recognition_of_Kosovo "International recognition of Kosovo") as an independent country*), [Liechtenstein](/wiki/Liechtenstein "Liechtenstein"), [Luxembourg](/wiki/Luxembourg "Luxembourg"), [Malta](/wiki/Time_in_Malta "Time in Malta"), [Monaco](/wiki/Monaco "Monaco"), [Montenegro](/wiki/Montenegro "Montenegro"), [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands"), [North Macedonia](/wiki/North_Macedonia "North Macedonia"), [Norway](/wiki/Time_in_Norway "Time in Norway"), [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland"), [San Marino](/wiki/San_Marino "San Marino"), [Serbia](/wiki/Serbia "Serbia"), [Slovakia](/wiki/Slovakia "Slovakia"), [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia"), [Spain](/wiki/Time_in_Spain "Time in Spain") (except the [Canary Islands](/wiki/Canary_Islands "Canary Islands")), [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden"), [Switzerland](/wiki/Time_in_Switzerland "Time in Switzerland") and [Vatican City](/wiki/Vatican_City "Vatican City").[[3]](#cite_note-cetusage-3)

#### History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Time&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: History")]

* 1884
  + The [Kingdom of Serbia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Serbia "Kingdom of Serbia") starts using CET.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
* 1 October 1891
  + [Austro-Hungarian Empire](/wiki/Austro-Hungarian_Empire "Austro-Hungarian Empire") adopts CET. At first railways and post offices, cities such as Prague and Budapest, but not Vienna.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-otfa-7) (present-day [Austria](/wiki/Austria "Austria"), [Czech Republic](/wiki/Czech_Republic "Czech Republic"), [Croatia](/wiki/Croatia "Croatia"), [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary"), [Slovakia](/wiki/Slovakia "Slovakia"), [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia") and some other regions)
* 1 April 1893
  + The [German Empire](/wiki/German_Empire "German Empire") unified its time zones to use CET (MEZ).[[7]](#cite_note-otfa-7)
  + [Malta](/wiki/Malta "Malta")[[8]](#cite_note-8) uses CET.
  + Vienna (then part of [Austro-Hungarian Empire](/wiki/Austro-Hungarian_Empire "Austro-Hungarian Empire")) starts using CET.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* 1 November 1893
  + [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy") starts using CET.[[10]](#cite_note-10)
* 1894
  + Switzerland switches from [UTC+00:30](/wiki/UTC%2B00:30 "UTC+00:30") to CET[[11]](#cite_note-11)
  + Liechtenstein introduces CET.[[12]](#cite_note-12)
  + Denmark adopts CET.[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* 1895
  + Norway adopts CET.[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* 1900
  + Sweden adopts CET.[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* 1904
  + Luxembourg introduces CET,[[16]](#cite_note-16) but leaves 1918.[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* 1914
  + Albania adopts CET.[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* 1914–1918
  + During [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I") CET was implemented in all German-occupied territories.[[19]](#cite_note-thetimenow-19)
* 1920
  + Lithuania adopts CET (but subsequently rescinded in 1940) and 1998−1999 again.[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* 1922
  + Poland adopts CET.[[21]](#cite_note-21)
* 1940
  + Under German occupation:[[19]](#cite_note-thetimenow-19)
    - The Netherlands was switched from [UTC+00:20](/wiki/UTC%2B00:20 "UTC+00:20") to CET.
    - Belgium was switched from [UTC+00:00](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00").
    - Luxembourg was switched from [UTC+00:00](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00").
    - [France](/wiki/Time_in_France "Time in France"), which had adopted Paris time on 14 March 1891 and Greenwich Mean Time on 9 March 1911,[[22]](#cite_note-22) was switched to CET.
  + [Spain](/wiki/Time_in_Spain "Time in Spain") switched to CET.[[19]](#cite_note-thetimenow-19)

After World War II [Monaco](/wiki/Monaco "Monaco"), [Andorra](/wiki/Andorra "Andorra") and [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar") implemented CET.[[19]](#cite_note-thetimenow-19)

[Portugal](/wiki/Time_in_Portugal "Time in Portugal") used CET in the years 1966–1976 and 1992–1996.

United Kingdom

The time around the world is based on [Universal Coordinated Time](/wiki/Universal_Coordinated_Time "Universal Coordinated Time") (UTC) which is roughly synonymous with [Greenwich Mean Time](/wiki/Greenwich_Mean_Time "Greenwich Mean Time") (GMT). From late March to late October, clocks in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") are put forward by one hour for [British Summer Time](/wiki/British_Summer_Time "British Summer Time") (BST). Since 1997, most of the European Union aligned with the British standards for BST.

In 1968[[23]](#cite_note-23) there was a three-year experiment called *British Standard Time*, when the UK and [Ireland](/wiki/Republic_of_Ireland "Republic of Ireland") experimentally employed [British Summer Time](/wiki/British_Summer_Time "British Summer Time") (GMT+1) all year round; clocks were put forward in March 1968 and not put back until October 1971.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

Central European Time is sometimes referred to as [continental time](/wiki/Continental_time "Continental time") in the UK.

### Other countries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Time&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Other countries")]

Several African countries use [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") all year long, where it is known as [West Africa Time](/wiki/West_Africa_Time "West Africa Time") (WAT), although [Algeria](/wiki/Algeria "Algeria"), [Morocco](/wiki/Morocco "Morocco") and [Tunisia](/wiki/Tunisia "Tunisia") use the term *Central European Time* despite being in [North Africa](/wiki/North_Africa "North Africa").[[3]](#cite_note-cetusage-3)

Between 2005 and 2008, [Tunisia](/wiki/Tunisia "Tunisia") observed [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time").[[25]](#cite_note-25) [Libya](/wiki/Libya "Libya") also used CET during the years 1951–1959, 1982–1989, 1996–1997 and 2012–2013.

For other countries see [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") and [West Africa Time](/wiki/West_Africa_Time "West Africa Time").

## Discrepancies between official CET and geographical CET

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Time&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Discrepancies between official CET and geographical CET")]

| Colour | Legal time vs local mean time |
| --- | --- |
|  | 1 h ± 30 min behind |
|  | 0 h ± 30 min |
|  | 1 h ± 30 min ahead |
|  | 2 h ± 30 min ahead |
|  | 3 h ± 30 min ahead |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/09/Tzdiff-Europe-winter.png/330px-Tzdiff-Europe-winter.png)](/wiki/File:Tzdiff-Europe-winter.png)

European winter

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8e/Tzdiff-Europe-summer.png/330px-Tzdiff-Europe-summer.png)](/wiki/File:Tzdiff-Europe-summer.png)

European summer

The criteria for drawing time zones is based on many factors including: legal, political, economic, and physical or geographic. Consequently, time zones rarely adhere to meridian lines. The CET time zone, were it drawn by purely geographical terms, would consist of exactly the area between meridians 7°30′ E and 22°30′ E. As a result, there are European locales that despite lying in an area with a "physical" or "nominal" [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") time, actually use another time zone ([UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") in particular – there are no "physical" [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") areas that employ [UTC+00:00](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00")). Conversely, there are European areas that have gone for [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00"), even though their "physical" time zone is UTC (typically), [UTC−01:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9201:00 "UTC−01:00") (westernmost [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain")), or [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") (e.g. the very easternmost parts of [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"), [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden"), [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland") and [Serbia](/wiki/Serbia "Serbia")). On the other hand, people in Spain still usually have work and meal hours one hour later than France and Germany despite sharing the same time zone.[[26]](#cite_note-26) Historically [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar") maintained [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") all year until the opening of the [land border with Spain](/wiki/Gibraltar%E2%80%93Spain_border "Gibraltar–Spain border") in 1982, when it followed its neighbour and introduced CEST. The following is a list of such "incongruences":

### Areas within [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") longitudes using other time zones

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Time&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Areas within UTC+01:00 longitudes using other time zones")]

These areas are between 7°30′ E and 22°30′ E ("physical" UTC+1)[[27]](#cite_note-tzmap1-27)[[28]](#cite_note-tzmap2-28)

#### Areas using [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Time&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Areas using UTC+02:00")]

* The westernmost part of [Greece](/wiki/Greece "Greece"), including the cities of [Patras](/wiki/Patras "Patras"), [Ioannina](/wiki/Ioannina "Ioannina") and the island of [Corfu](/wiki/Corfu "Corfu")
* The westernmost parts of the [Bulgarian](/wiki/Bulgaria "Bulgaria") provinces of [Vidin](/wiki/Vidin "Vidin") and [Kyustendil](/wiki/Kyustendil "Kyustendil")
* The westernmost part of [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), including most of the area of the counties of [Caraș-Severin](/wiki/Cara%C8%99-Severin_County "Caraș-Severin County"), [Timiș](/wiki/Timi%C8%99_County "Timiș County") (capital [Timișoara](/wiki/Timi%C8%99oara "Timișoara")), [Arad](/wiki/Arad_County "Arad County"), and [Bihor](/wiki/Bihor_County "Bihor County"), as well as the westernmost tips of the counties of [Mehedinți](/wiki/Mehedin%C8%9Bi_County "Mehedinți County") and [Satu Mare](/wiki/Satu_Mare_County "Satu Mare County")
* The westernmost tip of [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"), near the border with [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") and [Slovakia](/wiki/Slovakia "Slovakia"), at the Ukrainian [Transcarpathian Oblast](/wiki/Transcarpathian_Oblast "Transcarpathian Oblast") ([Zakarpattia Oblast](/wiki/Zakarpattia_Oblast "Zakarpattia Oblast")), essentially comprising the city of [Uzhhorod](/wiki/Uzhhorod "Uzhhorod") and its environs. (Although CET is used as local, non-official time in [Transcarpathia](/wiki/Carpathian_Ruthenia "Carpathian Ruthenia")).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
* Western [Lithuania](/wiki/Lithuania "Lithuania"), including the cities of [Klaipėda](/wiki/Klaip%C4%97da "Klaipėda"), [Tauragė](/wiki/Taurag%C4%97 "Tauragė"), and [Telšiai](/wiki/Tel%C5%A1iai "Telšiai")
* Western [Latvia](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia"), including the cities of [Liepāja](/wiki/Liep%C4%81ja "Liepāja") and [Ventspils](/wiki/Ventspils "Ventspils")
* The westernmost parts of the [Estonian](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia") islands of [Saaremaa](/wiki/Saaremaa "Saaremaa") and [Hiiumaa](/wiki/Hiiumaa "Hiiumaa"), including the capital of the [Saare County](/wiki/Saare_County "Saare County"), [Kuressaare](/wiki/Kuressaare "Kuressaare")
* The southwestern coast of [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"), including the cities of [Turku](/wiki/Turku "Turku"), [Pori](/wiki/Pori "Pori"), and [Vaasa](/wiki/Vaasa "Vaasa"); also the [Åland](/wiki/%C3%85land "Åland") islands (of Finnish jurisdiction) – the [Åland](/wiki/%C3%85land "Åland") islands are the westernmost locale applying EET in the whole of Europe
* The northwesternmost part of [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"), including [Kilpisjärvi](/wiki/Kilpisj%C3%A4rvi "Kilpisjärvi") and [Kaaresuvanto](/wiki/Kaaresuvanto "Kaaresuvanto").
* The [Russian](/wiki/Russia "Russia") exclave of [Kaliningrad Oblast](/wiki/Kaliningrad_Oblast "Kaliningrad Oblast"), excluding however its easternmost slice (the city of [Nesterov](/wiki/Nesterov "Nesterov") is east of 22°30′ E, but that of [Krasnoznamensk](/wiki/Krasnoznamensk,_Kaliningrad_Oblast "Krasnoznamensk, Kaliningrad Oblast") is not)

### Areas outside [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") longitudes using [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00") time

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Time&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Areas outside UTC+01:00 longitudes using UTC+01:00 time")]

These areas are either west of 7°30′ E or east of 22°30′ E (outside nominal [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00"))[[27]](#cite_note-tzmap1-27)[[28]](#cite_note-tzmap2-28)

#### Areas between 22°30′ W and 7°30′ W (nominal [UTC−01:00](/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9201:00 "UTC−01:00"))

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Time&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Areas between 22°30′ W and 7°30′ W (nominal UTC−01:00)")]

* The westernmost part of mainland [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain") ([Galicia](/wiki/Galicia_(Spain) "Galicia (Spain)"), e.g. the city of [A Coruña](/wiki/A_Coru%C3%B1a "A Coruña")); [Cape Finisterre](/wiki/Cape_Finisterre "Cape Finisterre") and nearby points in Galicia, at 9°18′ W, are the westernmost places of CET in Spain.
* The Norwegian island of [Jan Mayen](/wiki/Jan_Mayen "Jan Mayen") lies entirely within this area and extends nearly as far west as Cape Finisterre, with its western tip at 9°5′ W and its eastern tip at 7°56′ W.
* Western [Morocco](/wiki/Morocco "Morocco") including the city of [Casablanca](/wiki/Casablanca "Casablanca"), at 7°35′ W. CET usage in Morocco extends as west as 13°10′ W.
* The entirety of [Western Sahara](/wiki/Western_Sahara "Western Sahara") with its western tip at 17°6′ W and its eastern tip at 8°40′ W.

#### Areas between 7°30′ W and 7°30′ E (nominal [UTC+00:00](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00"))

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Time&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Areas between 7°30′ W and 7°30′ E (nominal UTC+00:00)")]

* [Andorra](/wiki/Andorra "Andorra")
* [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium")
* [France](/wiki/France "France"), with the small exception of two separate easternmost parts of the mainland, one along eastern [Alsace](/wiki/Alsace "Alsace"), incl. [Strasbourg](/wiki/Strasbourg "Strasbourg") and the other in parts of the [Alpes-Maritimes](/wiki/Alpes-Maritimes "Alpes-Maritimes") department, as well as the island of [Corsica](/wiki/Corsica "Corsica"). Overseas departments of France use local times.
* The very westernmost part of [Germany](/wiki/Germany "Germany"), incl. the cities of [Saarbrücken](/wiki/Saarbr%C3%BCcken "Saarbrücken"), [Düsseldorf](/wiki/D%C3%BCsseldorf "Düsseldorf"), [Cologne](/wiki/Cologne "Cologne"), [Bonn](/wiki/Bonn "Bonn"), [Aachen](/wiki/Aachen "Aachen"), and [Trier](/wiki/Trier "Trier")
* The absolutely westernmost part of [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy"), incl. the cities of [Aosta](/wiki/Aosta "Aosta") in [Aosta Valley](/wiki/Aosta_Valley "Aosta Valley") and [Cuneo](/wiki/Cuneo "Cuneo") in [Piedmont](/wiki/Piedmont "Piedmont")
* [Luxembourg](/wiki/Luxembourg "Luxembourg")
* [Monaco](/wiki/Monaco "Monaco")
* [Netherlands](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands")
* The westernmost part of [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"), incl. the cities of [Bergen](/wiki/Bergen,_Norway "Bergen, Norway") and [Stavanger](/wiki/Stavanger "Stavanger")
* [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"), except for the westernmost part of the mainland (see above) and the [Canary Islands](/wiki/Canary_Islands "Canary Islands") (which are further than 7°30′ W and use [UTC+00:00](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00")).
* [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar")
* The part of [Switzerland](/wiki/Switzerland "Switzerland") west of [Bern](/wiki/Bern "Bern") (inclusive), also incl. cities such as [Basel](/wiki/Basel "Basel"), [Geneva](/wiki/Geneva "Geneva"), [Lausanne](/wiki/Lausanne "Lausanne"), and [Fribourg](/wiki/Fribourg "Fribourg")
* Most of [Algeria](/wiki/Algeria "Algeria") including its capital [Algiers](/wiki/Algiers "Algiers").

#### Areas between 22°30′ E and 37°30′ E (nominal [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00"))

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Central_European_Time&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Areas between 22°30′ E and 37°30′ E (nominal UTC+02:00)")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8c/Petsamo.png/250px-Petsamo.png)](/wiki/File:Petsamo.png)

Map of [Petsamo area](/wiki/Pechengsky_District "Pechengsky District") in northern Finland/Soviet Union/Russia. The green area is the Finnish part of the [Rybachi peninsula](/wiki/Rybachy_Peninsula "Rybachy Peninsula") (Kalastajasaarento) which was ceded to the Soviet Union after the [Winter War](/wiki/Winter_War "Winter War"). The Red area is the [Jäniskoski-Niskakoski](/wiki/J%C3%A4niskoski-Niskakoski_territory "Jäniskoski-Niskakoski territory") area ceded to the USSR in 1947.

* The easternmost part of [North Macedonia](/wiki/North_Macedonia "North Macedonia"), including the city of [Strumica](/wiki/Strumica "Strumica")
* The easternmost part of [Serbia](/wiki/Serbia "Serbia"), in the [Pirot District](/wiki/Pirot_District "Pirot District"), including the city of [Pirot](/wiki/Pirot "Pirot"), and small easternmost parts of [Bor District](/wiki/Bor_District "Bor District").
* The easternmost tips of [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") and [Slovakia](/wiki/Slovakia "Slovakia"), bordering to the north and south respectively the Ukrainian [Transcarpathian Oblast](/wiki/Transcarpathian_Oblast "Transcarpathian Oblast") ([Zakarpattia Oblast](/wiki/Zakarpattia_Oblast "Zakarpattia Oblast")), a bit to the east of [Vásárosnamény](/wiki/V%C3%A1s%C3%A1rosnam%C3%A9ny "Vásárosnamény"), [Hungary](/wiki/Hungary "Hungary") – [Uzhhorod](/wiki/Uzhhorod "Uzhhorod"), [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine") (both at 22°18′ E) line
* The easternmost part of [Poland](/wiki/Poland "Poland"), including the cities of [Lublin](/wiki/Lublin "Lublin") and [Białystok](/wiki/Bia%C5%82ystok "Białystok")
* The northeast of [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden"), in the [Norrbotten province](/wiki/Norrbotten "Norrbotten"), including the cities of [Kalix](/wiki/Kalix "Kalix") and [Haparanda](/wiki/Haparanda "Haparanda")
* The northeast of [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"), lying north of [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"), roughly coinciding with the county of [Finnmark](/wiki/Finnmark "Finnmark"). The easternmost town in [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"), [Vardø](/wiki/Vard%C3%B8_(town) "Vardø (town)"), lies at 30°51′ E, which is so far east, so as to be east even of the central meridian of EET ([UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")), i.e. east of [Istanbul](/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul") and [Alexandria](/wiki/Alexandria "Alexandria"). The sun reaches its highest point at 10:56 (when not DST), although the sun does not vary so much in height at the latitude 70°N.   
  The [Norwegian](/wiki/Norway "Norway")–[Russian](/wiki/Russia "Russia") and the [Polish](/wiki/Poland "Poland")-[Belarusian](/wiki/Belarus "Belarus") border are the only places where CET (UTC+1/+2) borders Moscow time ([UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00")), resulting in a two hours time change (or one hour in summer) for the travellers crossing that border.
* There is a "tri-zone" point (where [UTC+01:00](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00"), [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00"), and [UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00") meet, winter times) at the [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway")–[Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland")–[Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia") tripoint near [Muotkavaara](/wiki/Muotkavaara "Muotkavaara"). During the summer Finland and Russia both have [UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00").
